name: Interfaith
description_short: Interfaith Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving faith-based organizations and individuals with savings, checking, loans, and specialized pastoral lending programs.
description_long:
Interfaith Federal Credit Union (IFCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union headquartered in Montclair, California, operating under NCUA insurance. The organization has positioned itself as a financial institution specifically designed to serve faith communities, churches, and individuals aligned with religious organizations. This mission-driven focus differentiates it from traditional banks and mainstream credit unions.

IFCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including savings accounts (Purpose Savings), checking accounts (including youth accounts starting at age 13), IRAs, and investments. On the lending side, they provide auto loans (as low as 5.49% APR for new vehicles), home mortgages, home equity lines of credit, Visa credit cards (as low as 11.75% APR), and signature loans. They uniquely emphasize specialized pastoral loan programs and church-specific products like the Church Visa and Church Corporate Elite Visa designed for faith organization expenditure management. Members also access Zelle money transfer, skip-a-pay options, and nationwide access through the CO-OP network.

What distinguishes IFCU is its explicit focus on faith communities rather than general consumer banking. They offer dedicated Church Loans (as low as 7.25% APR), Faith Organization Loans, Online Giving services, and a "Jubilee Initiative." Their pastoral loan programs appear tailored to clergy and religious professionals. The credit union also bundles TruStage insurance products offering auto, property, life, and accidental death & dismemberment coverage with member discounts. Educational resources include budgeting worksheets, calculators, fraud protection guidance, and what they call "3 Steps to Financial Freedom."

IFCU operates as a legitimate credit union with standard compliance features including online banking, mobile banking, fraud protection, and secure document portals. However, prospective members should note that membership eligibility appears tied to faith affiliation or participation in eligible religious organizations, which may limit accessibility compared to open-membership credit unions. Rate competitiveness appears reasonable but requires comparison shopping, and the emphasis on faith-based services means secular consumers may find better options elsewhere.
services: Savings accounts (Purpose Savings) and checking accounts for individuals and youth (age 13+)
Auto loans (new vehicles from 5.49% APR; used from 6.99% APR)
Home mortgages and home equity lines of credit
Visa credit cards (consumer cards from 11.75% APR; Church Corporate Elite Visa for organizations)
Specialized pastoral loan programs for clergy and religious professionals
Church Loans and Faith Organization Loans (from 7.25% APR)
Online and mobile banking with bill pay and loan payment options
TruStage insurance products (auto, property, life, AD&D) with member discounts
CO-OP network nationwide ATM and shared branching access
Skip-a-pay loan deferment options and online loan applications
pros: Specialized pastoral loan programs and clergy-focused financial products not widely available elsewhere
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.49% APR for new vehicles and 6.99% for used
Church-specific products like Church Corporate Elite Visa streamline faith organization finances
Member access to nationwide CO-OP network ATMs and shared branching beyond physical locations
Zelle integration for member-to-member money transfers with fraud protection features
Youth checking accounts available starting at age 13 with debit card and Zelle access
TruStage insurance bundle provides auto, property, life, and AD&D coverage discounts
cons: Membership eligibility likely restricted to faith-affiliated individuals or faith organization employees, limiting accessibility
Visa credit card APR of 11.75% is higher than many competing credit cards and banks
Limited branch network concentrated in California; primarily reliant on online/mobile banking and CO-OP shared branching
No information provided about savings account APY rates, making deposit competitiveness unclear
Marketing heavily emphasizes faith mission which may alienate secular consumers seeking standard credit union services
best_for: Clergy, religious professionals, and pastoral staff seeking specialized financing aligned with faith values
Faith-based organizations and churches needing dedicated corporate lending and expense management solutions
Religious community members seeking mission-aligned financial services from a faith-focused institution
Individuals seeking auto loans at competitive rates with faith community connection and values alignment
diagnosis: Interfaith Federal Credit Union is best for clergy, faith organization employees, and religiously-affiliated individuals seeking specialized pastoral lending and mission-aligned financial services. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted to faith community members, and secular consumers should verify their eligibility before pursuing membership—traditional or secular-focused credit unions may offer better rates and broader accessibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Interfaith — NCUA-insured credit union in MONTCLAIR, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
